name: a scoped v1.1 profile carrying families_active, project_id and project_overlay_namespaces validates against the schema
command: ID=FEAT-560E; bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/acquire-technology-rules.sh" --technology Vue --project $ID --source-file g.txt --source-id sid --now 2026-07-05T00:00:00Z >/dev/null 2>&1; bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/full-surface-intake.sh" --workload "a dashboard with Vue" --answers p.json --project $ID --partial --out prof.json --now 2026-07-05T00:00:00Z >/dev/null 2>&1; node "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/detectors/lib/validate-json-schema.js" "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/schemas/business-profile.schema.json" prof.json && ruby -rjson -e 'p=JSON.parse(File.read("prof.json")); exit(p["families_active"]==["frontend"] && p["project_id"]=="'$ID'" && p["project_overlay_namespaces"]==["vue"] ? 0:1)' < prof.json && rm -rf "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/generated-code-quality-standards"/_project/$ID && echo scoped-profile-valid 1>&2 || { rm -rf "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/generated-code-quality-standards"/_project/$ID; false; }
setup: printf '%s\n' 'Always bind a unique key to v-for.' > g.txt
